lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Good day to you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And to you. What brings you all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I am on my return journey from the dark lands of Morytania. It would be remiss of me not to stop and take in the glory of Paterdomus before my return to Entrana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Morytania? That's quite a dangerous place for a monk.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Indeed, but as a faithful servant of Saradomin, it is my duty to confront the taint of Zamorak, wherever it may be found. Violence is to be abhorred, but at times it is an unfortunate necessity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well I'd better leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Before you depart, adventurer, I was wondering if you might be interested in helping me with something?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Good day to you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Did you reconsider my request? I'd be very grateful if you'd be able to help me with something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: On my travels in Morytania, I encountered an individual from the lands to the far east. He spoke of a weapon from his homeland that I think would be very valuable in our fight against the darkness.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: If you could help me build one of these weapons, you would be highly rewarded by the Church of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A new weapon? Interesting!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Yes, now pay attention. I need you to take a two-handed mithril sword and grind it down to a slender blade. I know of a dwarf living north of Falador whose whetstone is unmatchable. It will be perfect for this task.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Once the blade is flexible enough, you should then be able to string it with a normal bow string. That is all you need to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back once I have this weapon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm busy right now.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Ah, well do come and see me if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. Have you brought the weapon for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, sorry.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Remember, you need to take a two-handed mithril sword and grind it down to a slender blade. There is a dwarf living north of Falador whose whetstone will be perfect for this.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Once the blade is flexible enough, you should then be able to string it with a normal bow string. That is all you need to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. Have you brought the weapon for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it right here for you.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Excellent! Your help won't be forgotten.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Now, I think it's high time for me to make my return to Entrana. There is a special ceremony taking place soon, and to miss it would fill me with regret.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: On that note, there might be something else you can help me with...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I have a special gift I wish to present at the ceremony. It's one that I know my fellow monks will appreciate. As such, it feels only right that a special gift is delivered in a special way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I would like my companions to enter the ceremony with no knowledge of the gift. It will then make for a joyous surprise for them. However, to do that, it must be smuggled onto the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Smuggled? Sounds a bit shifty.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I confess, to do so would not quite be in keeping with our rules, but for such a wonderful gift, the opportunity can't be disregarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough. So you want me to sneak this gift onto the island? How do I do that?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Our island is home to the Runic Altar of Law. Recently, I have heard whispers of a way to access these altars via another realm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You speak of the Abyss. I'm familiar with it.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Excellent! In that case, please take this orb and journey through this 'Abyss' to the island of Entrana. Please do keep the orb safe though, for it is highly delicate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Abyss isn't exactly the safest of places.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: The same voices that whispered of this 'Abyss' also spoke of pouches used to store runes. I'm sure a large one of those would be suitable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get that done.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Wonderful! Here is the orb. Once you arrive, just place it on the altar in Entrana's church.

sections.title: Talking the Monk after receiving the orb

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Hello again. You need to hurry or you'll miss the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to get there again?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Use this 'Abyss' you spoke of to travel to the Runic Altar of Law. When doing so, make sure to put the orb in a large rune pouch to keep it safe.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Once you arrive, just place the orb on the altar in Entrana's church.

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Hello again. You need to hurry or you'll miss the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I lost the orb...

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: You're very lucky that I found it. Please be more careful with it this time.

sections.title: Putting the orb in a pouch
Checking the orb filled large pouch
Emptying the orb filled large pouch
Trying to place the orb on the altar
Placing the pouch on the altar

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Protect the relic!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: You won't have it!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The relic! This is a disaster!

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Adventurer, did you see who put that teleport beacon on the altar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah, about that...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I put it there...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There was this monk near Paterdomus. He told me it was a gift...

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: No worshipper of Saradomin would ever do something like this! Adventurer, you must return to Paterdomus and see if you can find any trace of this 'monk'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go right away.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest again

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Adventurer, have you found that 'monk' yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Please hurry to Paterdomus and see if you can find any trace of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't good. I'd better get back and tell the High Priest.

sections.title: Returning to the High Priest in Entrana

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Adventurer, have you found that 'monk' yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I went back to Paterdomus, but all I found was a dead body.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looked like he'd been killed by magic. The thing is, I'm not convinced he was the person I spoke to before.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: This is not good. That relic wasn't just for show! It was once part of a powerful artefact. You must report this to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll head there right away.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Jolly good show on getting into the White Knights, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now how can I help you old [boy/girl/bean]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some bad news...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: This wouldn't be about that old relic being stolen from Entrana, would it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah, how'd you know?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Part of the business, old bean. So what do you know about what went on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there was this monk who needed help...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... and so I was told to come see you.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good, that confirms our own intel. We will get to work at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Not yet, but we'll be in touch if we need you, old bean. For now, you should be on your way, but don't worry, we'll get to the bottom of this!
